checks.a_analysis: The output text has been carefully compared against the source PDF. All headers, meeting minutes, agenda items, and descriptions correspond to content found in the original document. No invented sections, introductions, or other fabricated text was detected.

The phone number (203-704-0581) present in the output accurately reflects the source document (Pages 34-36). No additional or fabricated phone numbers, email addresses, or URLs were found in the output. While some contact information was omitted, nothing was hallucinated.

The output JSON skips several complete pages of content that are not covered by the 'omitted' block (which targeted the brochure pages 7-27). Specifically, Page 34 (RAK scenario-based training description and objectives) and Page 36 (Training Payment Options and COVID-19 precautions) are missing entirely. The reading order jumps from the end of the brochure comparison (Page 28) directly to the dispatcher objectives (Page 35), and then from Page 35 to the committee goals (Page 37).
checks.b_evidence_source: Entire content of Page 34 (starting with 'Dispatch Response to Active Killers') and Page 36 (starting with 'Training Payment Options').
checks.b_evidence_output: The content from Page 34 and Page 36 is absent from the sequence of sections and elements.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections following element index 357 (Page 28) and preceding element index 489 (Page 37).

checks.a_analysis: Major tables in the output, such as the Education Committee Meeting Agenda (Page 1), the Attendance Table (Page 3), and the Curriculum Overview Comparison (Page 28), all contain the correct number of rows and columns as compared to the source PDF. Minor reformatting, such as moving bullet points from a table cell to a sub-list, has preserved all data rows.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The output contains significant text omissions in the contact section for Dan Jewiss on Page 35. The email address (D.Jewiss@me.com) and the website URL (www.TheRAKAcademy.com) are both omitted, leaving the sentence truncated and incomplete. This represents a loss of essential contact information for the program described.
checks.b_evidence_source: Please contact Dan Jewiss at D.Jewiss@me.com or 203-704-0581 with any questions or to host this training. www.TheRAKAcademy.com
checks.b_evidence_output: Please contact Dan Jewiss at ... or 203-704-0581 with any questions or to host this training.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: A minor text truncation occurs at the end of the first paragraph of Item 8 in the meeting minutes. The text is cut off mid-word, which is a defect in the transcription fidelity.
checks.b_evidence_source: ...approve the 911 4 Heroes Telecommunicator Training Bundle Online subscription at the current course list for the subscription at 90%.
checks.b_evidence_output: ...approve the 911 4 Heroes Telecommunicator Training Bundle Online subscription at the current co subscription at 90%.

checks.a_analysis: I examined the document for repeated text blocks. The agenda, minutes, and goals sections are represented once. Specific rows in tables, like 'JD Hartman' in the CMCP budget table, appear twice in the source and are correctly reflected twice in the output. No instances of accidental pipeline duplication were found.

The heading hierarchy contains several major breaks. In the Minutes section, the document title is set as H1 (index 14), but the subsequent section headers (e.g., '6. Technology Update') are set as H3 (index 20, 25, 26), skipping the H2 level. Additionally, in the Curriculum Overview Comparison section (index 355-356), an H2 precedes an H1, which is logically inverted hierarchy. These defects hinder navigation for assistive technologies.
checks.b_evidence_output: {"type": "heading", "level": 3, "text": "6. Technology Update..."} (index 20); {"type": "heading", "level": 2, "text": "RQI Telecommunicator CPR"} followed by {"type": "heading", "level": 1, "text": "Curriculum Overview Comparison"} (index 355-356)

checks.a_analysis: In the Agenda table on Page 1, the content for Tab 8 includes sub-bullets ('Buncombe County...', 'Haywood County...'). The output extracts these bullets from the table cell and places them in a separate list element (index 8) after the table. This corrupts the structural integrity of the table as the specific data associated with Tab 8 is no longer contained within that row's structure.
checks.b_evidence_source: Tab 8 Topic cell containing: 'Buncombe County: RQI Telecommunicator CPR Program...' and 'Haywood County 911: RAK Academy...' (Page 1)
checks.b_evidence_output: The table data cell for Tab 8 only contains the introductory text (index 7, row 9), while the specific program details are moved to a separate list element (index 8) following the entire table.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order is materially broken by the extraction of sub-bullets from the Agenda table. Because the table element (index 7) contains all 13 agenda items and is processed before the subsequent list (index 8), a reader would encounter agenda items 9 through 13 before reading the sub-details for item 8. This breaks the logical flow of the meeting agenda.
checks.b_evidence_source: Agenda item 8 sub-bullets are presented immediately following the item 8 header within the table flow. (Page 1)
checks.b_evidence_output: Table (reading_order 7) includes items 1-13. List (reading_order 8) contains item 8 details. Item 9-13 text appears in output before item 8 bullet details.

checks.a_analysis: The numbered lists for the '2022 Education Committee Goals' and '2023 Education Committee Goals' on Page 37 have been converted to unordered lists in the output. While the items themselves are preserved, the numbering (1, 2, 3, 4) used in the source to denote priority or sequence is lost.

The output fails to preserve functional contact information. In Section 35, the email address 'D.Jewiss@me.com' present in the source footer is entirely missing from the output text, leaving a trailing preposition with no object.
checks.b_evidence_source: Please contact Dan Jewiss at D.Jewiss@me.com or 203-704-0581 (Page 35)
